quiz.title: General Knowledge Quiz
quiz.description: Test your knowledge across a range of topics!

questions.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet, with a mass more than twice that of all other planets combined.

questions.question: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: Earth has 7 continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.

questions.question: Which element has the chemical symbol 'Au'?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum', meaning gold.

questions.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between approximately 1503 and 1519.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo has been the capital of Japan since 1869, when Emperor Meiji moved the Imperial seat there.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in a vacuum (approximately)?
questions.explanation: Light travels at approximately 299,792 km/s, commonly rounded to 300,000 km/s.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest, covering more than 30% of Earth's surface.

questions.question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, marking a pivotal moment in the end of the Cold War.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond scores 10 on the Mohs hardness scale, making it the hardest naturally occurring substance.

questions.question: How many strings does a standard guitar have?
questions.explanation: A standard guitar has 6 strings, tuned E-A-D-G-B-E from lowest to highest pitch.
